_description: Phase 5 Azure-Lily DIMM-top residual cycle deltas between the Regime-A analytical sim (gemm_dsp with DSP_WDITH=4) and the cycle-accurate RTL, annotated with file:line root causes. A per-stage delta present here is accepted by run_checks.py (DIMM latency path) as long as |delta - expected_delta_cycles| <= expected_delta_tolerance.

_schema.delta_cycles: RTL - sim (negative = RTL faster than sim)
_schema.root_cause: file:line citation of the structural or FSM-handshake source of the residual

note: Phase 5 (2026-04-20) — Azure-Lily DIMM parity with NL-DPE Phase 3+4. The mac_qk / mac_sv stages share a structural residual: RTL dsp_mac (azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:152-215) fires 5 products/cycle (4 int_sop_4 DSP + 1 CLB multiply, documented in gen_dimm_azurelily_top.py's dsp_mac generator), so per-row cycles = ceil(d/5)=13 (mac_qk) and ceil(N/5)=26 (mac_sv). Sim's gemm_dsp (azurelily/IMC/peripherals/fpga_fabric.py:170-230) uses DSP_WDITH=4 strict (scheduler_stats/common.py:6), yielding k_tile=16 and k_tile=32 respectively. RTL is therefore ~18.75% faster than sim on every DSP stage; the softmax stage inherits this speed-up across (N-1)=127 streamed scores giving its dominant contribution. Stage-name mapping to AL modules: Score→mac_qk, Softmax→clb_softmax (S_LOAD+S_INV+S_NORM), Wsum→mac_sv.

deltas.root_cause: Minor 1-cycle residual between RTL's 15-cyc first-row window (K=13 valid cycles in dsp_mac @ azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:104-113 + 2-cyc SRAM read pipeline fill, gated by mac_count>=2 on v:107) and sim's k_tile_qk=ceil(d/DSP_WDITH)=16 cyc per-row compute. The sim over-counts by one because it models k_tile as a pure compute window, whereas the RTL pipelines the SRAM read during the first 2 cycles before dsp_mac.valid asserts.

Dominant structural delta from the 5-product/cycle fusion in dsp_mac (azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:150-215: int_sop_4 DSP fires 4 products/cycle at lines 179-189, plus the implicit CLB multiplier's 5th product packed into the 40-bit word format documented in the module header, lines 6-7). RTL per-row = 13+2 setup = 15 cyc; sim per-row = k_tile_qk=16 cyc. Across the softmax probe window the remaining (N-1)=127 rows stream into clb_softmax's S_LOAD (clb_softmax @ azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:274-282), so the accumulated delta = 127 * (sim_per_row - RTL_per_row) ≈ 127 * 1 = 127 cyc faster on RTL. The +1 cycle from the score stage delta re-enters here because sim's softmax_start anchors at sim's (longer) score end, not at the actual mac_qk first-row-out moment. Residual ~126 cyc matches this within the ±8 tolerance.

Structural DSP-width residual on mac_sv. RTL dsp_mac with K=26 (azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:125, K = ceil(N/EPW=5) = 26 packed pairs per output element) produces first out_valid after 26 valid cycles; sim models k_tile_sv = ceil(N/DSP_WDITH) = ceil(128/4) = 32 cycles per output. Delta = RTL 26 - sim 32 = -6 cycles, matching the same 4-vs-5-products-per-cycle mismatch as the score / softmax stages. Classification structural: aligned with fc_verification/results/azurelily_dimm_alignment_log.txt "=== Phase E — mac_sv ===" (RTL 28 vs sim 64 per-col under a slightly different compile-time configuration; same root cause).

Additive sum of per-stage residuals: score (-1) + softmax (-126) + wsum (-6) + ~1 cyc rounding at stage boundaries = -132 cyc. Classification follows the dominant component (softmax), which is structural. For the paper the Azure-Lily RTL is ~6.3% faster than the sim predicts on end-to-end DIMM latency — the sim conservatively under-predicts Azure-Lily's performance when translating DSP_WDITH=4 cycles into wall-clock time.
